name: Stephen Foster
abstract: Stephen Collins Foster (July 4, 1826 – January 13, 1864), known as "the father of American music", was an American composer known primarily for his parlour and folk music during the Romantic period. Foster wrote more than 200 songs, including "Oh!

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: We had spirituals and art songs. Like "Carry Me Back to Old Virginia", and a lot of, like Stephen Foster's stuff, "Way Down Upon the Swanny River".

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Israels
about_quotes.evidence: The things that had built — on which our popular music was built, going back to Stephen Foster and the music my grandmother sang to me
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Israels interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 11, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: let alone an Irving Berlin song or Cole Porter or Stephen Foster for that matter.

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Steinau
about_quotes.evidence: she also had several old song type collections you know Stephen Foster things

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Crow
about_quotes.evidence: You know, Stephen Foster songs, World War I songs, stuff like that.
